heading: Forfeiture
label: 1954 Forfeiture
text: No yearly renewable term insurance or United States Government life insurance shall be payable for death inflicted as a lawful punishment for crime or military offense, except when inflicted by the enemy. In such cases the cash surrender value of United States Government life insurance, if any, on the date of such death shall be paid to the designated beneficiary if living, or if there be no designated beneficiary alive at the death of the insured the said value shall be paid to the estate of the insured.
notes: Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–83section 754 of this title
amendments: Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–83section 754 of this title
